methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Pre-Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $6M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Solideon operates in Robotics & Manufacturing at the Pre-Seed stage, having raised $6.5M to date. The broader sector comprises one public comparable (277810.KQ) and eight private peers including 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics. However, the peer set lacks sufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables to support a reliable peer-median approach—only one narrow-filter peer set yielded insufficient data points for statistical confidence.

The Stage-Progression method is the primary applicable triangulation approach. Using $6.5M in total raised as a value proxy and applying a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of Pre-Seed to next-stage progression yields an implied valuation of $16M. This reflects standard venture capital advancement patterns across the pre-seed to seed transition. The peer-median method was excluded due to insufficient narrow-stage comparables within Robotics & Manufacturing; a broader all-sector median would introduce unacceptable sectoral heterogeneity.

Analysis supports a valuation range of $14M (low) to $19M (median $16M), anchored to stage-based progression mechanics. Key uncertainties include the absence of direct peer benchmarking within the same stage and sub-sector, limiting confidence in the implied multiple. Any refinement would require either additional Pre-Seed robotics comparables or closer examination of the broader peer universe's stage distribution and exit multiples.
